title: Game Audio Implementation
creator: Richard Stevens and Dave Raybould
description: A practical implementation guide built around exercises in game engines and audio middleware. It covers triggers, spatial sound, interactive music, dialogue, mixing, optimization, and troubleshooting.
useful: It focuses on getting sound into a functioning game, bridging a common gap between asset creation and technical implementation.
audience: Sound designers and technical audio students moving from a digital audio workstation into game tools.
learning: Implement events, ambience, and spatial sound
Build interactive music and dialogue systems
Test and optimize audio inside an engine
